basics.name: Tassia Corcoran
basics.label: AI Solutions Analyst | M.S. Information Systems | Researcher in Artificial Intelligence & Information Literacy
basics.summary: Technology professional with a strong foundation in generative AI, data systems, and enterprise IT strategy. Experienced in developing AI policy, designing training programs, and leading CRM data reconciliation and integration across academic, nonprofit, and defense-related environments. Skilled in translating complex technical concepts into practical solutions that improve workflows, support responsible AI use, and enhance organizational decision-making.

education.institution: University of Hawaii at Mānoa, Honolulu, HI
University of Hawaii at Mānoa, Honolulu, HI

work.summary: AI data analytics and solutions role focused on large-scale dataset validation and generative AI deployment.
highlights: Maintain and validate large-scale datasets (500,000+ records) to ensure accuracy, completeness, and readiness for analytics and reporting
Conduct data discovery and gap analysis, defining requirements and sourcing missing information to support operational decision-making
Develop measurement frameworks, dashboards, and assessment plans to track performance, investments, and outcomes across multiple projects
Analyze multi-source data to produce executive-ready insights, trends, and recommendations for stakeholders
Built and deployed a secure generative AI chatbot restricted to approved internal data, improving information retrieval speed and consistency for end users

work.position: Curriculum Developer – MSIS Program (AI & Cloud Computing)
work.summary: Designed graduate-level curriculum for the Master's in Information Systems program.
highlights: Designing graduate-level curriculum for the Master's in Information Systems program, focused on artificial intelligence and enterprise applications
Developing technical modules covering AWS cloud architecture, certification preparation, and applied machine learning concepts
Creating hands-on labs and case studies to train students in cloud-based AI workflows and enterprise integration strategies

work.summary: Led AI implementation and data management initiatives.
highlights: Collaborated with internal departments to design and lead company-wide training on Artificial Intelligence tools and concepts, creating use cases to evaluate AI-generated outputs for accuracy, reliability, and business applicability
Successfully reconciled and validated over 100,000 records during Maui Strong donation processing, ensuring system-wide data accuracy across platforms
Co-authored and implemented AI policy guidelines to govern responsible AI use across organizational departments, considering both generative AI and machine learning capabilities in the workplace
Cleaned, consolidated, and integrated large-scale relational datasets across multiple CRM platforms to improve data integrity and reporting
Worked with third-party APIs to determine export methods for ingesting data from diverse external sources into organizational databases, streamlining data flow and supporting advanced reporting

work.summary: Led lectures on networking and security fundamentals.
highlights: Led lectures on foundational networking concepts, including LAN/WAN architecture, TCP/IP protocols, wireless standards, and network security principles
Guided students through hands-on exercises in configuring and analyzing network components, fostering practical understanding of enterprise networking and cybersecurity frameworks
Graded and evaluated assignments, exams, and projects for 50+ upper-level MIS students, ensuring fair assessment and reinforcing key concepts in IT infrastructure design and threat mitigation

work.summary: DoD Pacific Intelligence & Innovation Initiative (P3I) cyber-spectrum operations internship.
highlights: Participated in the DoD Pacific Intelligence & Innovation Initiative (P3I) through a 7-week cyber-spectrum operations internship
Worked under the Shipyard Infrastructure Optimization Program (SIOP) by researching digital twin technologies for Pearl Harbor Navy Shipyard's (PHNSY) maintenance operations
Led AI implementation seminars, training professionals on practical use cases, policy, and ethical integration
Built a geodatabase that improved the Navy's ability to track hazardous waste, raw materials, and over $10M in equipment

Supported a multibillion-dollar waterfront production infrastructure project by analyzing 7,000+ data points across 4 reports using advanced Excel functions; identified 38+ unique equipment discrepancies, calculated square footage gaps, and visualized misalignments to guide decision-making

work.summary: Teaching and guiding students in computer fundamentals and programming concepts.
highlights: Facilitated classes of 45 students on HTML, CSS, UNIX, and Microsoft programs such as Word, Excel, PowerPoint, and Access
Designed and delivered hands-on training sessions introducing students to generative AI tools, responsible use, and prompt engineering
Developed practical exercises combining AI literacy with technical skills, including HTML, CSS, UNIX, and Microsoft Office applications
Mentored a team of 5 assistants during open lab hours, providing guidance on complex technical issues and instructional support
Collaborated with faculty to integrate AI literacy into core coursework and evaluate its effectiveness
Successfully supported over 400 students through hands-on troubleshooting, technical support, and targeted business software training

work.summary: Conducting IRB-approved research on generative AI in education.
highlights: Conducted IRB-approved research on student proficiency, ethics, and perceptions of generative AI through multi-phase studies
Evaluated prompt engineering strategies for content generation and information queries
Applied ACRL standards to evaluate AI tool use in student writing and information literacy development
Co-authored and presented a peer-reviewed paper on integrating generative AI into education at an international conference

work.summary: Providing expert consulting services and customized training seminars.
highlights: Provided expert consulting services to companies, delivering customized training seminars on Microsoft programs and computer usage to management employees
Designed and facilitated interactive workshops, enhancing employees' proficiency in key Microsoft tools and improving overall productivity
Designed transition-oriented curricula tailored to adult learners unfamiliar with office productivity tools

work.summary: Supply chain risk management for SOCPAC contracts.
highlights: Focused on supply chain risk management (SCRM) for SOCPAC contracts within the Export/Logistics division
Enhanced SCRM processes by utilizing Microsoft Power Automate to streamline file management and schedule critical tasks, including RIP (Request to Initiate Purchases) closeouts
Collaborated with onsite teams to gather and analyze logistical data essential for contract fulfillment

mentorships.position: Mentee of Robert Redding, Vice President and Chief Technology Officer, HMSA
highlights: Explored enterprise AI integration for information risk management
Observed technical leadership discussions around security, AI adoption, and enterprise IT risk controls

mentorships.position: Mentee of Preston Robler, Systems Automation and Security Manager, BOH
highlights: Acquired comprehensive knowledge of IT services within the bank, including working with and updating Microsoft 365 on the back end and assisting employees with any issues regarding changes

activities.organization: National Center for Women in Technology (NCWIT)
activities.summary: Advancing diversity in tech at UH Mānoa.
highlights: Established the NCWIT chapter at UH Mānoa to advance diversity in tech
Led outreach events promoting technology careers and academic support
Mentored students in tech courses and advised on career pathways
Collaborated with campus leadership and industry partners to drive inclusion in computing fields

awards.title: Dean's List
awards.summary: Recognized for achieving a GPA of 3.5 or higher each semester.

publications.name: Information Literacy: A Foundational Approach to Generative AI Competence
publications.publisher: EdMedia World Conference on Educational Technology
publications.summary: Presented at the EdMedia World Conference on Educational Technology, Barcelona, Spain, May 2025. Co-authored with C. Olsen.
